join_order.build: qualifying_orderkeys(vector<int32_t>, ~2520 entries from merged int16_t array)
join_order.notes: Direct O(1) index lookup for each of the ~2520 qualifying orderkeys. orders_by_orderkey[okey] = row index into orders column files. Completely eliminates the 15M-row sequential orders scan and the 57MB is_big[] char array.
Dense array index 5.7MB fully L3-resident; only ~2,520 lookups — negligible cost.

filters.predicate: SUM(l_quantity) GROUP BY l_orderkey HAVING SUM > 300
filters.notes: Pass 1 HAVING filter; ~2,520 qualifying orderkeys identified after parallel merge of int16_t thread-local arrays.

data_structures.pass1_qty_accumulator: thread_local_array(int16_t, size=60000001, 120MB virtual per thread); parallel morsel scan; no atomics; int16_t sufficient because max merged value per orderkey = 7 lineitems × 50 qty = 350 < 32767; 32 entries per 64-byte cache line vs 16 for int32_t, halving cache-line pressure and merge bandwidth
data_structures.qualifying_keys: std::vector<int32_t>(~2520 entries); built during merge phase by scanning merged int16_t[60000001] array (120MB sequential, ~0.6ms); each merge thread appends qualifying keys from its key-space slice to a thread-local vector, then all vectors are concatenated
data_structures.orders_direct_lookup: orders_by_orderkey dense_array(int32_t[60000001], 240MB, mmap O_RDONLY + MADV_RANDOM); orders_by_orderkey[okey] = row index into orders column files; 2520 random accesses into page-cached index replaces 300MB sequential orders scan

parallelism.pass1_lineitem_scan: parallel morsel scan; each thread owns thread-local int16_t[60000001] (calloc); each thread accumulates l_quantity into its local array indexed by l_orderkey; no atomics, no shared state; wait for all threads before merge
parallelism.merge_and_collect: parallel element-wise merge into tl_qty[0]: each merge thread handles a key-space slice [lo, hi); within its slice it adds tl_qty[1..nthreads-1] into tl_qty[0], then scans tl_qty[0] in [lo, hi) and appends qualifying keys (value > 300) to a thread-local qualifying_keys vector; after parallel phase, concatenate all per-thread qualifying_keys vectors
parallelism.pass2_direct_lookup: sequential over ~2520 qualifying keys; no parallelism needed (trivial work)

indexes.notes: File: orders/orders_by_orderkey.bin. Layout: int32_t[60000001], array[okey] = row index into orders column files, sentinel -1 for unused. Used for direct O(1) lookup of qualifying orders rows. 240MB file; MADV_RANDOM; 2520 cache-miss accesses = 252μs total. In page cache for hot runs.
File: customer/customer_by_custkey.bin. Layout: int32_t[1500001], array[ckey] = row index into customer column files, sentinel -1. 5.7MB, fully L3-resident. 2520 lookups total.

fields.name: o_orderdate
fields.source: orders/o_orderdate.bin[orders_by_orderkey[okey]] (days_since_epoch; decode to YYYY-MM-DD for output only)

fields.name: sum_qty
fields.source: cast(tl_qty[0][okey], int32_t) from merged int16_t array

optimization_notes: Two combined optimizations from iter_0. (1) Category A — plan restructuring: replace the 15M-row sequential orders scan with direct index lookup. After Pass 1, qualifying orderkeys (~2520) are known. The pre-built orders_by_orderkey.bin index (int32_t[60000001], 240MB) maps each orderkey to its orders row in O(1). Iterating over 2520 qualifying keys and issuing direct row lookups costs ~252μs, replacing a 300MB sequential scan plus the 57MB is_big[] char array construction. The is_big[] array (57MB) was also an LLC-miss source in the orders scan since it exceeds L3 (44MB); eliminating both removes ~5-6ms from Pass 2. (2) Category B — algorithm change: switch thread-local accumulators from int32_t (229MB per thread) to int16_t (120MB per thread). int16_t is safe because the maximum sum per orderkey is 7 lineitems × 50 max_qty = 350, well within int16_t range (32767). This halves the physical memory written per thread (~1.83MB vs ~3.66MB) and halves the merge read bandwidth (63 × 1.83MB vs 63 × 3.66MB physical), while keeping per-thread independence and avoiding the atomic false-sharing contention that caused iter_1 to regress. The qualifying_keys collection is folded into the merge pass (single sequential scan of the 120MB merged array), adding only ~0.6ms.
